---
# High-risk login geography from JSON payload risk scores (T1078)

**Dataset**: `gx_04_raw_json`

Surfaces countries whose authentication events carry an elevated average risk score, working from a raw JSON `payload`. It pulls a nested scalar (`$.geo.country`) and coerces the string `$.risk` value to an integer with `to_integer` so numeric aggregates work, then applies a HAVING-style post-aggregate `filter avg_risk >= 40` to keep only the suspicious geographies. Gotcha embodied: `json_extract_scalar` returns a string, so wrap it in `to_integer` before any `avg`/`max`; the second `filter` runs *after* `comp`, acting on the computed alias.

```sql
dataset = gx_04_raw_json
| alter country = json_extract_scalar(payload, "$.geo.country"), risk = to_integer(json_extract_scalar(payload, "$.risk"))
| filter risk != null
| comp avg(risk) as avg_risk, max(risk) as max_risk, count() as events by country
| filter avg_risk >= 40
| sort desc avg_risk
```
